expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
expectations.evidence: The review notes '29 files, 960 insertions / 8 deletions' and 'Medium frontend feature with backend endpoints' but does not include any explicit complexity advisory or suggestion to split the PR.

expectations.text: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
expectations.evidence: The CRITICAL finding explicitly states: 'IUserService.UpdateCurrentUserAsync added without default implementation' and references 'Per the project's breaking change policy (CLAUDE.md section 5.3)'. A code example with NotImplementedException is provided.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering
expectations.evidence: Several cosmetic findings: 'label attribute binding inconsistency' (string interpolation vs property binding), 'Trailing blank line + whitespace in UserService.cs', 'Test variable named modal instead of model' (typo), 'Unused form wrapper'. These are stylistic nitpicks.

expectations.text: All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
expectations.evidence: All findings reference code introduced in this PR: new interface methods, new SDK methods, new repository code, new modal elements, new controller, new test file. No pre-existing issues flagged.

expectations.text: Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach -- not just absence of bugs
expectations.evidence: The review includes architectural assessments: 'Condition logic inverted -- SectionUserNoPermission is unnecessary' (suggesting the existing condition could gain a negate property), 'CreateUpdateCurrentUserModelAsync is needlessly async', the const ABI concern for AddCms, and the discussion of multiple independent observers vs passing data as properties.
